verses.text: Solomon, strengthened in his kingdom with the LORD his God magnifying him, leads all Israel to worship at Gibeon, where the old tabernacle stood. He begins his reign by seeking God. The wise start of any work is to put the worship of God first.

Solomon, strengthened in his kingdom with the LORD his God magnifying him, leads all Israel to worship at Gibeon, where the old tabernacle stood. He begins his reign by seeking God. The wise start of any work is to put the worship of God first.

Solomon, strengthened in his kingdom with the LORD his God magnifying him, leads all Israel to worship at Gibeon, where the old tabernacle stood. He begins his reign by seeking God. The wise start of any work is to put the worship of God first.

At the brazen altar Solomon offers a thousand burnt offerings. His devotion is lavish, holding nothing back. Those whom God would use greatly are those who first seek Him greatly and worship Him without reserve.

At the brazen altar Solomon offers a thousand burnt offerings. His devotion is lavish, holding nothing back. Those whom God would use greatly are those who first seek Him greatly and worship Him without reserve.

At the brazen altar Solomon offers a thousand burnt offerings. His devotion is lavish, holding nothing back. Those whom God would use greatly are those who first seek Him greatly and worship Him without reserve.

God appears: 'Ask what I shall give thee,' and Solomon, feeling the weight of his task, asks not for wealth or long life but 'wisdom and knowledge' to judge God's people. The best prayer asks for what we need to serve God and others well.

God appears: 'Ask what I shall give thee,' and Solomon, feeling the weight of his task, asks not for wealth or long life but 'wisdom and knowledge' to judge God's people. The best prayer asks for what we need to serve God and others well.

God appears: 'Ask what I shall give thee,' and Solomon, feeling the weight of his task, asks not for wealth or long life but 'wisdom and knowledge' to judge God's people. The best prayer asks for what we need to serve God and others well.

God appears: 'Ask what I shall give thee,' and Solomon, feeling the weight of his task, asks not for wealth or long life but 'wisdom and knowledge' to judge God's people. The best prayer asks for what we need to serve God and others well.

God, pleased with the unselfish request, grants wisdom and adds riches, wealth, and honour Solomon did not ask. Seek first the things of God, and the rest is added. He who asks rightly receives both his request and more from a generous God.

God, pleased with the unselfish request, grants wisdom and adds riches, wealth, and honour Solomon did not ask. Seek first the things of God, and the rest is added. He who asks rightly receives both his request and more from a generous God.

Solomon returns to reign, gathering chariots and horses and silver like stones. The promised wealth pours in — yet horses 'out of Egypt' hint at the law's warning. Prosperity, even God-given, must be held with a watchful heart lest it lead astray.
